id="9a7b" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">More and more projects today are looking for transparent and reliable ways to collaborate with authors on X. This is exactly why <strong class="nm hp">Rally</strong> was created — a protocol designed to simplify the process of rewarding genuine, verifiable publications. It ensures that projects can trust their funds are not wasted but spent on proven contributions. The logic of the system is built to minimize manipulation and manual arrangements — saving both budget and effort. All actions are recorded on-chain, without intermediaries and with clear, transparent rules.</p><p id="0589" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">On the homepage, this is stated very clearly: anyone with an X account and their own audience can participate and earn, while verification and payouts happen automatically through <strong class="nm hp">GenLayer</strong> <strong class="nm hp">smart contracts</strong>. The workflow is also described: you join a campaign, complete a mission on X, the system evaluates the result, and transfers the reward. Everything is transparent!</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx paragraph-image"><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="nf ng fv nh bd ni"><span class="ga gb gc ai gd ge gf fo gg id="5371" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Now let’s take a closer look: what the protocol is, why it was created, how the evaluation system works, what roles participants can take, how blockchain is used, and what is important to know before getting started. Below is an overview based entirely on <strong class="nm hp">Rally’s official materials</strong>.</p><h2 id="dab7" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">What is Rally</h2><p id="935d" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally is an on-chain marketing protocol for X. Projects launch campaigns with missions, and authors participate through tweets, threads, and replies. Content and engagement are then verified using several AI models and strictly defined rules. Rewards are distributed automatically by smart contracts. The core idea is highly practical: pay for <strong class="nm hp">proven contributions</strong>, not for promises or flashy price tags. The website states clearly that the protocol is open to anyone with a voice and a community. Participation is free — all you need is an X account and a wallet to receive rewards.</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx paragraph-image"><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="nf ng fv nh bd id="fb41" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">On August 26, 2025, Rally’s blog published an introductory article where the team explained why the classic model of closed deals, informal agreements in chats, and reliance on superficial metrics like follower counts does not work. Rally offers an alternative: <strong class="nm hp">programmable participation rules, transparent quality assessment, and on-chain settlements</strong>. The article also notes that at the end of each period, campaign scores are aggregated into a unit of account called <strong class="nm hp">Rally Points</strong>, which serve as the basis for payouts according to the campaign’s rules.</p><h2 id="65ba" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Who is behind this</h2><p id="fa29" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The service is provided by Rally Foundation. On all pages, this organization is listed as the site owner and the one setting the terms. In the documentation, the combination of Rally and GenLayer is repeatedly mentioned. The first part (Rally) is responsible for the application, interface, EVM contracts, and distribution of funds in campaigns. The second part consists of GenLayer smart contracts, which verify that content complies with the rules, assess quality</p><p id="1b0f" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">and engagement, and help reach consensus on evaluations. In the documentation, this is described as <strong class="nm hp">“built on GenLayer.”</strong></p><p id="1ddc" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp"><em class="ps">“</em></strong><em class="ps">Multi-LLM Consensus: Submissions are verified by multi-LLM consensus on</em><strong class="nm hp"><em class="ps"> GenLayer”</em></strong></p><h2 id="9679" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">How Rally works inside</h2><p id="bc4d" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The website has a short section that outlines the flow of actions. There are no applications or manual moderation. First, a campaign is selected, then a post is published on X, after which <strong class="nm hp">GenLayer smart contracts</strong> check whether the author has met the conditions. If so, the payment is sent on-chain. The same page also explains which technologies are used: GenLayer for evaluation and verification, <strong class="nm hp">LayerZero</strong> for omnichain reward routing, <strong class="nm hp">Base</strong> for low-cost and fast settlements, and <strong class="nm hp">ZKsync</strong> for scalability and security.</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx paragraph-image"><div id="c690" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The blog provides a more detailed scenario. The application functions as a marketplace. A project sets the budget, duration, AI knowledge base, and mission requirements. Creators choose missions and publish content. The system evaluates each submission and assigns <strong class="nm hp">campaign points</strong>. <strong class="nm hp">Payouts happen automatically</strong>, and all the rules are visible in advance, which reduces misunderstandings between the parties.</p><ul class=""><li id="c6c5" class="nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh pu pv pw bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">AI‑Verified Scoring:</strong> Multiple models independently score every submission, eliminating single points of failure and preventing gaming.</li><li id="89b4" class="nk nl ho nm b nn px np nq nr py nt nu nv pz nx ny nz qa ob oc od qb of og oh pu pv pw bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">Programmable Targeting:</strong> Projects precisely control participation through open access, verified-only, follower thresholds, or curated whitelists.</li><li id="105d" class="nk nl ho nm b nn px np nq nr py nt nu nv pz nx ny nz qa ob oc od qb of og oh pu pv pw bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">Onchain Settlement (via Intelligent Contracts):</strong> Smart contracts automatically release funds only for verified content, with every payout and metric recorded onchain.</li><li id="3348" class="nk nl ho nm b nn px np nq nr py nt nu nv pz nx ny nz qa ob oc od qb of og oh pu pv pw bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">Multi‑Chain:</strong> Campaigns run seamlessly across Base and zkSync Era, with more chains coming soon.</li><li id="4b5a" class="nk nl ho nm b nn px np nq nr py nt nu nv pz nx ny nz qa ob oc od qb of og oh pu pv pw bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">Scalability:</strong> Scale from 10 to 10,000 creators without additional coordination overhead, as the protocol handles everything automatically.</li></ul><h2 id="4cd6" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Post Evaluation and Reward Distribution</h2><p id="ac66" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">This part is the most interesting. The documentation explains in detail how contribution is calculated. First, a publication goes through <strong class="nm hp">four entry checks</strong>. The content must match the campaign theme, be factually correct, comply with the rules, and maintain originality and the author’s voice. If it <strong class="nm hp">is not counted</strong> at any of these thresholds, the work is not counted. If it passes, a <strong class="nm hp">quality multiplier</strong> is applied, which depends on the average scores across these four thresholds.</p><p id="771e" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Next come the softer metrics and live signals. Quality includes engagement potential and the technical quality of the text. Dynamic metrics include retweets, likes, replies, reply quality as assessed by AI, and the number of followers of those who reply. These values are normalized, with engagement counts using a <strong class="nm hp">logarithmic scale</strong> to penalize empty manipulation and prevent runaway advantage from sheer volume. The <strong class="nm hp">weights (0–1)</strong> of each metric are visible in the campaign brief, so the author understands exactly what matters.</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx id="03ec" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">If a campaign is split into several periods, <strong class="nm hp">engagement refresh</strong> is available. In the next period, the author locks in the <strong class="nm hp">only growth</strong> of reactions relative to the previous point rather than summing everything from scratch. This prevents double counting and closes many loopholes. Finally, a distribution curve is applied, which determines the concentration of rewards. The <strong class="nm hp">alpha parameter</strong> controls this curve and is written directly into the formula.</p><div class="tq tr ts tt v j cs"><div class="tu tv tw tx ty e fj"><h2 class="tz b ua ub uc ud ue">Get Crouton Digital’s stories in your inbox</h2></div><div class="cp e fj"><p class="bb b bc u dy">Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.</p></div><div class="ui ri uj uk rh ul v"><span class="bb b bc u bg"><div class="um v cs"><div class="v um fg ff bl un uo db lq up uq ur us ut uu uv"><input class="uw af ac ah ux uy gg le bh uz bd" placeholder="Enter your email" type="text" value=""></div></div></span><div class="au bt ry bx"><div class="uf ug uh"><button class="bb b bc u va qj vb vc vd ve vf ez fa vg vh vi fe ff fg fh bi fi fj">Subscribe</button></div></div><div class="bd by r s"><button class="bb b bc u va qj vb vc vd ve vf ez fa vg vh vi fe bd ff fg fh bi fi fj">Subscribe</button></div></div><div class="su e"><label class="v j"><div class="vy vz wa v wb bx fv"><input class="vj vk vl ga vm vn vo vp vq vr vs vt" type="checkbox" checked=""><span class="j vu vv ff fg vw fh va ak v vb vx cf" data-checkbox="true"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="11" height="11" viewBox="0 0 11 11" class="vb"><path d="m0 6.313 3.704 3.705.904.904.66-1.095 5.296-8.795L8.85 0 3.554 8.795l1.563-.191-3.704-3.705z"></path></svg></span></div><div class="e"><p class="bb b bc u bg">Remember me for faster sign in</p></div></label></div><div class="qh e"><div id="g-recaptcha"></div></div></div><p id="2acf" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn np nq nr nt nu nv nx ny nz ob oc od of og tt oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">In the blog, these principles are restated in plain language: first quality thresholds, then the combination of a <strong class="nm hp">fixed part</strong> for content value and a <strong class="nm hp">dynamic part</strong> for real audience reaction. Engagement refresh in new periods ensures that only growth is counted. Everything is transparent and reproducible.</p><h2 id="e60d" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Roles & Use Scenarios</h2><p id="8eaf" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">1) Author<br></strong>Entry is simple: connect your <strong class="nm hp">X account</strong> and <strong class="nm hp">wallet</strong>, choose a campaign with suitable missions, and publish content. The system checks compliance and assigns points automatically. The FAQ states participation is <strong class="nm hp">free</strong> (no access fees) — you only need connected accounts. Read the <strong class="nm hp">campaign brief</strong>: it shows <strong class="nm hp">metric weights</strong> and <strong class="nm hp">quality thresholds</strong>.</p><p id="38a0" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph=""><strong class="nm hp">2) Project<br></strong>Creates the <strong class="nm hp">campaign</strong>, sets the <strong class="nm hp">reward pool</strong>, uploads a <strong class="nm hp">knowledge base for AI</strong>, configures <strong class="nm hp">metric weights</strong> and the <strong class="nm hp">distribution curve</strong>. The protocol then collects submissions, evaluates them, and <strong class="nm hp">distributes payouts</strong>. During <strong class="nm hp">alpha</strong>, a partner program offers <strong class="nm hp">zero fees</strong>, <strong class="nm hp">token matching</strong>, and <strong class="nm hp">priority placements</strong>.</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx paragraph-image"><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="nf ng fv nh bd ni"><span class="ga gb gc ai gd ge gf fo gg id="391e" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Multichain Architecture</h2><p id="1b6c" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The technical docs outline the overall plan. <strong class="nm hp">EVM contracts</strong> handle campaign creation, escrow of funds, distribution, X-identity verification, and multichain deployment. <strong class="nm hp">GenLayer smart contracts</strong> perform text evaluation, engagement verification, anti-spam, and dynamic reward calculations. State is synchronized across networks via a <strong class="nm hp">LayerZero-based bridge</strong>. This setup combines familiar EVM security with <strong class="nm hp">machine evaluation where regular contracts fall short</strong>, enabling richer logic.</p><p id="368a" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">On the homepage there’s a dedicated tech section listing GenLayer, LayerZero, Base, and ZKsync — not just logos. For participants, <strong class="nm hp">low transaction costs and the ability to move rewards across networks</strong> matter; for projects, scalability and protocol-level verifiability are key.</p><h2 id="d567" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally Points & How Calculations Work</h2><p id="9b34" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">During <strong class="nm hp">alpha</strong>, rewards are accounted in <strong class="nm hp">Rally Points</strong> — a <strong class="nm hp">settlement unit</strong> that campaign points convert into at the final stage. Accruals are made <strong class="nm hp">at the end of each period</strong>, and the <strong class="nm hp">rules live in smart contracts</strong> and are visible upfront. Campaign rewards may be paid in <strong class="nm hp">different tokens</strong> (e.g., USDC or a project token), depending on the organizer’s choice.</p><h2 id="aac7" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Referral System</h2><p id="3c76" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The logic is simple. You earn <strong class="nm hp">10% of invited authors’ campaign points</strong> — and this applies to <strong class="nm hp">every</strong> campaign they run, not just the first. For invited projects, you earn <strong class="nm hp">10% of the Rally Points distribution</strong> allocated to their campaigns. Referral links are taken from the <strong class="nm hp">waitlist</strong>, and attribution is <strong class="nm hp">permanent</strong>. This mechanism rewards those who grow the interface between the protocol and real users.</p><figure class="oj ok ol om on ne mw mx paragraph-image"><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="nf ng fv nh id="fb0f" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">RLY Token & Its Role</h2><p id="5485" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">The site outlines RLY’s utility functions. It is used to <strong class="nm hp">unlock higher-tier campaigns</strong>, enable future staking and veRLY governance, provide voting rights over reward distribution and protocol upgrades, is <strong class="nm hp">not an investment instrument (no profit/share rights)</strong>, and its value is tied to participation in the protocol.</p><h2 id="2bf8" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rules & Restrictions</h2><p id="a749" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">There are a few things worth knowing in advance. The terms include a section on <strong class="nm hp">prohibited regions</strong> and an explicit <strong class="nm hp">ban on using VPNs to bypass geoblocks</strong>. If you’re in a restricted territory, you cannot use the service.</p><p id="687f" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally <strong class="nm hp">does not custody your funds or transact on your behalf</strong>. All transactions occur on the relevant network, <strong class="nm hp">are irreversible</strong>, and <strong class="nm hp">you connect your own wallet</strong> — so responsibility lies with you. The platform may charge <strong class="nm hp">service fees</strong>, and <strong class="nm hp">network fees</strong> are also on your side. Issues at the blockchain/network level are part of the <strong class="nm hp">user-accepted risk</strong>.</p><p id="fefb" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Another important point concerns Rally’s role between projects and authors. Unless Rally itself is the campaign owner, it <strong class="nm hp">is not a party to your private terms</strong>. Compliance with specific requirements and advertising rules (including disclosure guidelines) rests with the <strong class="nm hp">campaign parties</strong> — consistent with a neutral protocol rather than an agency.</p><h2 id="65f3" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Why It Matters for Authors</h2><p id="abb9" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally values not just audience size but <strong class="nm hp">substance and real interaction</strong>. The FAQ highlights <strong class="nm hp">fair scoring for smaller accounts</strong> with genuine engagement. In plain terms, a smart thread with relevant reader reactions can beat a hollow promo post with a hundred thousand silent followers. For creators, this is a <strong class="nm hp">predictable way to earn from your voice and expertise</strong> — without waiting for closed lists.</p><p id="f1eb" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">In practice, a simple discipline helps: read the <strong class="nm hp">campaign brief</strong> before you tweet (it shows sources and what the system looks at). Keep a <strong class="nm hp">dialogue in replies</strong> — both reply quality and the <strong class="nm hp">audience of repliers</strong> matter. If a campaign runs in periods, <strong class="nm hp">refresh engagement</strong> so only growth is counted. And don’t skimp on meaning: context-free reposts contribute little to quality, which is the <strong class="nm hp">foundation of final distribution</strong>.</p><h2 id="80a9" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Why It Matters for Projects</h2><p id="8cd2" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Teams hate paying for noise. Rally makes it <strong class="nm hp">predictable</strong>: you set <strong class="nm hp">metric weights</strong> and choose a <strong class="nm hp">distribution curve</strong>. Want a fast sprint that favors top performers? Use a more <strong class="nm hp">concentrated curve</strong>. Need broad community education? Choose a more <strong class="nm hp">even profile</strong>. Payouts follow clear rules, and <strong class="nm hp">on-chain attribution</strong> simplifies reporting and internal analysis.</p><p id="e554" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">There’s also an <strong class="nm hp">Alpha Partner Program</strong>: zero fees during alpha, token matching, promo priority, and the chance to influence the roadmap. For new protocols, it’s a way to arrive early and <strong class="nm hp">set campaign standards</strong> alongside the Rally team.</p><h2 id="6526" class="oo op ho bb oq or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Short Roadmap & Current Stage</h2><p id="6b73" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn pm np nq nr pn nt nu nv po nx ny nz pp ob oc od pq of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally is in <strong class="nm hp">private alpha</strong>: access opens in waves for active waitlist users, per priorities explained on the blog. After a few weeks, the plan is to open <strong class="nm hp">beta</strong>. This phase includes <strong class="nm hp">access points</strong> (score) and community activities.</p><p id="6ae3" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nk nl ho nm b nn no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh hh bg" data-selectable-paragraph="">Rally rewards <strong class="nm hp">real contribution, not loudness</strong>. Authors write with purpose and engage; projects define clear rules and honest priorities. The protocol handles the rest. If you’ve wanted marketing <strong class="nm hp">without back-room deals</strong>, this is where <strong class="nm hp">rules live in code</strong> and <strong class="nm hp">settlement lives on-chain</strong> — available now via the waitlist and alpha, with early bonuses, a partner program, and a clear access mechanism.</p></div></div></div></div></section></div></div></article></div><div This is exactly why Rally was created — a protocol designed to simplify the process of rewarding genuine, verifiable publications. It ensures that projects can trust their funds are not wasted but spent on proven contributions. The logic of the system is built to minimize manipulation and manual arrangements — saving both budget and effort. Below is an overview based entirely on Rally’s official materials.","hasDropCap":null,"dropCapImage":null,"markups":[{"__typename":"Markup","type":"STRONG","start":256,"end":282,"href":null,"anchorType":null,"userId":null,"linkMetadata":null}],"codeBlockMetadata":null,"iframe":null,"mixtapeMetadata":null},"Paragraph:a93a893e5961_6":{"__typename":"Paragraph","id":"a93a893e5961_6","name":"dab7","type":"H3","href":null,"layout":null,"metadata":null,"text":"What is Rally","hasDropCap":null,"dropCapImage":null,"markups":[],"codeBlockMetadata":null,"iframe":null,"mixtapeMetadata":null},"Paragraph:a93a893e5961_7":{"__typename":"Paragraph","id":"a93a893e5961_7","name":"935d","type":"P","href":null,"layout":null,"metadata":null,"text":"Rally is an on-chain marketing protocol for X. Projects launch campaigns with missions, and authors participate through tweets, threads, and replies. Content and engagement are then verified using several AI models and strictly defined rules. Unless Rally itself is the campaign owner, it is not a party to your private terms. In plain terms, a smart thread with relevant reader reactions can beat a hollow promo post with a hundred thousand silent followers. For creators, this is a predictable way to earn from your voice and expertise — without waiting for closed lists.","hasDropCap":null,"dropCapImage":null,"markups":[{"__typename":"Markup","type":"STRONG","start":40,"end":70,"href":null,"anchorType":null,"userId":null,"linkMetadata":null},{"__typename":"Markup","type":"STRONG","start":91,"end":124,"href":null,"anchorType":null,"userId":null,"linkMetadata":null},{"__typename":"Markup","type":"STRONG","start":307,"end":360,"href":null,"anchorType":null,"userId":null,"linkMetadata":null}],"codeBlockMetadata":null,"iframe":null,"mixtapeMetadata":null},"Paragraph:a93a893e5961_49":{"__typename":"Paragraph","id":"a93a893e5961_49","name":"f1eb","type":"P","href":null,"layout":null,"metadata":null,"text":"In practice, a simple discipline helps: read the campaign brief before you tweet (it shows sources and what the system looks at).
